report_name: Novel 'GemStuffer' Campaign Abuses RubyGems Repository as Data Exfiltration Channel

threat_actors.name: GemStuffer Actors
threat_actors.description: The unknown threat actors behind the GemStuffer campaign, who abused the RubyGems repository for data storage and exfiltration of scraped data from U.K. government websites.
threat_actors.goals: To scrape and store data from U.K. local government websites using the RubyGems repository as a free storage medium.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The GemStuffer campaign used the RubyGems package registry, a legitimate web service, as a channel to exfiltrate and store scraped data.

attack_patterns.name: Data Staged
attack_patterns.description: The malicious scripts collected scraped HTML from government websites and packaged it into valid .gem archives before uploading them to the RubyGems repository.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used the `gem push` command to upload scraped data packaged into .gem files to the RubyGems repository, effectively using its public CDN as a cloud storage solution.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The scripts within the malicious gems were designed to automatically scrape web pages from various U.K. local government portals.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Exfiltration
attack_patterns.description: The entire process of scraping, packaging, and uploading data was automated using scripts containing hardcoded API keys.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: The malicious scripts contained hardcoded API keys to authenticate to the RubyGems repository for publishing new packages.

malware.name: GemStuffer Scraper Script
malware.description: Scripts embedded in malicious RubyGems packages designed to scrape web pages from U.K. government portals and upload the content back to the RubyGems repository as new gems.

campaigns.name: GemStuffer
campaigns.description: A novel campaign that abused the RubyGems package registry as a data exfiltration and storage channel for scraped data from U.K. government websites.

identities.name: RubyGems
identities.description: The public package registry that was abused by the GemStuffer campaign as a data exfiltration and storage channel. The abuse likely contributed to a temporary suspension of new account registrations.

identities.name: U.K. local government portals
identities.description: The source of the data scraped by the GemStuffer campaign's malicious scripts.

infrastructure.name: RubyGems Exfiltration Channel
infrastructure.description: The RubyGems public repository and its CDN were abused as a free, distributed storage and exfiltration channel for the scraped data.

indicators.name: Hardcoded RubyGems API Key Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Hardcoded RubyGems API Key
id: 2f8b1c9a-7e4d-4f6a-8b3c-1d9e0a5b4c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of a hardcoded API key within a file, a technique used in the GemStuffer campaign to abuse the RubyGems package registry.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/gemstuffer-abuses-150-rubygems-to.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-14
tags:
  - attack.t1552.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    Contents|contains:
      - 'api_key:'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects hardcoded API keys within package source code, which is a strong signal of malicious or abusive intent as seen in the GemStuffer campaign.
